commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Another returns to that country whence he was banished, to perish when arrested, not now as being a Christian, but as being a criminal!

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Or a railer. The Greek here signifies one that does evil, or a malefactor.
Or as coveting the goods of others. The Greek rather signifies one curiously prying into the affairs of others, which Protestants translate a busy body. (Witham)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
